perturbation.original_text: The API shall be manufactured by HOVIONE at its facilities in accordance with all relevant current Good Manufacturing Practices ("cGMPs"), the Specifications, and Applicable Laws, and pursuant to HOVIONE's Drug Master File ("DMF"), prepared by HOVIONE and filed with the US FDA.
perturbation.changed_text: The API shall be manufactured by HOVIONE at its facilities in general alignment with current Good Manufacturing Practices ("cGMPs"), the Specifications, and Applicable Laws, and with consideration to HOVIONE's Drug Master File ("DMF"), prepared by HOVIONE and filed with the US FDA.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "in accordance with" with "in general alignment with" and "pursuant to" with "with consideration to" weakens the obligation. It implies a degree of flexibility, possibly leading to deviations from strict cGMP compliance, which is unacceptable under FDA regulations.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 21 CFR Part 210 and 211 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice regulations for drugs
perturbation.law_explanation: 21 CFR § 211.5 mandates that drug products be manufactured in 'strict compliance' with cGMP regulations. The modified text introduces ambiguity, suggesting flexibility rather than strict adherence, which is not permitted under FDA regulations. This weakens the assurance that the API will meet the required quality standards and could potentially render the API adulterated under the FD&C Act.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Federal Register :: Request Access Request Access Request Access Due to aggressive automated scraping of FederalRegister.gov and eCFR.gov, programmatic access to these sites is limited to access to our extensive developer APIs. If you are human user receiving this message, we can add your IP address to a set of IPs that can access FederalRegister.gov & eCFR.gov; complete the CAPTCHA (bot test) below and click "Request Access". This process will be necessary for each IP address you wish to access the site from, requests are valid for approximately one quarter (three months) after which the process may need to be repeated. Request Access for 72.74.68.69 An official website of the United States government. If you want to request a wider IP range, first request access for your current IP, and then use the "Site Feedback" button found in the lower left-hand side to make the request.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the API 'shall be manufactured...in accordance with all relevant current Good Manufacturing Practices.' The modified text weakens this obligation by stating the API 'shall be manufactured...in general alignment with current Good Manufacturing Practices.' The Law Explanation provided states that '21 CFR § 211.5 mandates that drug products be manufactured in 'strict compliance' with cGMP regulations.' The modification introduces ambiguity where the FDA requires strict compliance, thus creating a legal contradiction as it weakens the original obligation.

perturbation.original_text: HOVIONE shall advise INTERSECT in writing in advance of making any changes to the Product Specifications or any material changes in the methods, processes or procedures in manufacturing the API that could affect the quality, purity and/or physical properties of the API, any changes will be made according to the terms of the quality agreement between the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: HOVIONE should, where practical, advise INTERSECT of changes to the Product Specifications or material changes in the methods, processes or procedures in manufacturing the API that could affect the quality, purity and/or physical properties of the API, and such changes will be handled with regard to the quality agreement between the parties.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "shall advise" to "should, where practical, advise" weakens the mandatory notification requirement. The phrase 'handled with regard to' is much less stringent than 'made according to'. This could allow HOVIONE to make changes without proper notification or adherence to the quality agreement, potentially affecting the API quality.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 21 CFR § 314.70 - Supplements and other changes to an approved NDA
perturbation.law_explanation: 21 CFR § 314.70 requires that changes to an approved New Drug Application (NDA) be reported to the FDA, particularly those affecting product specifications. The changed text creates an ambiguity that may cause HOVIONE to not report changes which is in direct contradiction to the regulations. This is because HOVIONE may make a determination that it is "not practical" to share the changes with INTERSECT.

perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'shall advise,' creating a mandatory obligation for HOVIONE to notify INTERSECT of changes. The changed text weakens this to 'should, where practical, advise,' introducing ambiguity and discretion. According to 21 CFR § 314.70, changes to an approved NDA, especially those affecting product specifications, must be reported to the FDA. By making the notification discretionary ('where practical'), the changed text creates a loophole where HOVIONE might avoid reporting changes, directly contradicting the regulation's intent as the law explanation indicates. The scraped snippet 1 confirms the existence of 21 CFR 314.70, which implies an obligation related to changes and supplements of approved NDAs, which the change in text directly undermines.

perturbation.original_text: HOVIONE shall provide certificates of analysis to INTERSECT for each batch of API delivered under this Agreement. API shall have at least [*] remaining on the date of delivery.
perturbation.changed_text: HOVIONE will endeavor to provide certificates of analysis to INTERSECT for each batch of API delivered under this Agreement. API should ideally have at least [*] remaining on the date of delivery.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "shall provide" with "will endeavor to provide" turns a definite obligation into a non-committal intention. Similarly, replacing "shall have at least" with "should ideally have at least" weakens the requirement for the remaining shelf life of the API. This creates ambiguity as to whether certificates of analysis will be consistently provided, and whether API shelf life standards will always be met, potentially leading to substandard API being supplied.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 21 CFR § 211.165 - Testing and release for distribution.
perturbation.law_explanation: 21 CFR § 211.165(e) requires that there be appropriate laboratory testing, as necessary, of each batch of drug product required to be free of objectionable microorganisms. The certificates of analysis is a key instrument to this compliance; as such, if the manufacturer doesn't provide it, it can be found in violation with the law. Also, the remaining of API shelf life should be guaranteed. The changed text creates an ambiguity, as HOVIONE does not need to provide a certificate, and that it may not be able to prove the product's shelf life.

perturbation.justification: The modification weakens the obligation to provide certificates of analysis and ensure API shelf life, creating ambiguity that contradicts the requirement for appropriate laboratory testing as suggested by the Law Explanation. The original text uses "shall," indicating a definite obligation, while the modified text uses "will endeavor" and "should ideally," making the obligation non-committal and ambiguous. The change undermines the ability to prove the product's shelf life. Although the scraped snippets do not directly reference certificate of analysis, the Law Explanation implies that not providing such certificates could lead to a violation of 21 CFR § 211.165(e).
